teamObjective.explanation: Deterministically repaired ToM trace with objective finish_hand.

partnerBelief.summary: My partner (player 2) has 3 cards and shares team 0. In this endgame race, partner likely wants to facilitate my exit. With no public plays yet, I cannot know their exact strength, but they are likely to pass on a high single lead if they cannot comfortably beat it, preserving cards to cover later or block opponents.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (players 1 and 3) each have 3 cards, more than my 2. They will try to prevent me from going out. A low lead gives them a cheap chance to take control; a high lead forces them to spend a trump or a high card, or let me keep the lead.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading A♠ maximizes the chance I win this trick outright or force opponents to use real resources. If I win, I keep the lead and can play 2♠ next, giving me a direct path to exit. Even if an opponent covers with a trump, I have forced them to consume strength.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the weak 2♠ almost certainly gets beaten by any opponent with a minimal higher card or trump. I lose initiative and remain stuck with A♠, depending on someone else leading a single I can win later. That is risky in a race where opponents have more cards.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
